session.user:
Here's the complete audit report:
--- Branch Audit Report - March 1, 2026
Scale Summary
┌─────────────────────────────────┬───────┐ │ Category │ Count │ ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────┤ │ Remote branches │ 1,492 │ ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────┤ │ Local branches (excl stubs) │ ~290 │ ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────┤ │ Local-only branches (no remote) │ 58 │ ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────┤ │ Worktrees (total) │ 64 │ ├─────────────────────────────────┼───────┤ │ Open PRs │ 17 │ └─────────────────────────────────┴───────┘
AUTO-CLEANUP (7 branches)
Local-only, already merged to master. No unique work.
clone-master, worktree-remove-tmux-feature, plnd/delete-pr-sync-02-25-2104, plnd/fetch-plans-by-ids-02-25-0307, plnd/fix-address-dispatch-run-t-02-24-0154, plnd/fix-address-dispatch-tui-02-24-0157, plnd/fix-land-force-delete-02-24-0245
BLOCKING WORKTREES (4)
Closed PRs checked out in worktrees, blocking gt repo sync: - erk-slot-37 - PR#8195 CLOSED - erk-slot-54 - PR#8358 CLOSED - erk-slot-55 - PR#8431 CLOSED - migrate-to-batch-exec (non-slot) - PR#8407 CLOSED
STALE DETACHED HEAD WORKTREES (8 non-slot)
7 on master (safe to remove), 1 (doc-cleanup-plan-backend-7973) needs checking.
LOCAL-ONLY learn/async-learn BRANCHES (45)
27 learn/* + 18 async-learn/* with no remote tracking. Leftover session data.
ACTIVE SLOTS WITH OPEN PRs (4) - No action needed
ACTIVE SLOTS WITHOUT PRs (6) - Contain unpushed work, need review
REMOTE BRANCHES: 1,492 total
After cleanup, gt repo sync will handle branches with closed/merged PRs. Remaining learn/async-learn/session branches could be batch-deleted.
---
⏺ User declined to answer questions
❯ clone-master, worktree-remove-tmux-feature, plnd/delete-pr-sync-02-25-2104, plnd/fetch-plans-by-ids-02-25-0307, plnd/fix-address-dispatch-run-t-02-24-0154, plnd/fix-address-dispatch-tui-02-24-0157, plnd/fix-land-force-delete-02-24-0245
delete thes branches
⏺ Bash(git branch -D clone-master worktree-remove-tmux-feature plnd/delete-pr-sync-02-25-2104 plnd/fetch-plans-by-ids-02-25-0307 plnd/fix-address-dispatch-run-t-02-24-0154 plnd/fix-address-dispatch-tui-02-24-0157 plnd/fix-land-force-delete-02-24-0245) ⎿ Deleted branch clone-master (was e41d00d96). Deleted branch worktree-remove-tmux-feature (was c9c1e3e9a). Deleted branch plnd/delete-pr-sync-02-25-2104 (was 2bbcbfc67). Deleted branch plnd/fetch-plans-by-ids-02-25-0307 (was e41d00d96). Deleted branch plnd/fix-address-dispatch-run-t-02-24-0154 (was 33f9f060d). Deleted branch plnd/fix-address-dispatch-tui-02-24-0157 (was 33f9f060d). Deleted branch plnd/fix-land-force-delete-02-24-0245 (was fd64e81d1).
⏺ All 7 branches deleted.
❯ BLOCKING WORKTREES (4)
Closed PRs checked out in worktrees, blocking gt repo sync: - erk-slot-37 - PR#8195 CLOSED - erk-slot-54 - PR#8358 CLOSED - erk-slot-55 - PR#8431 CLOSED - migrate-to-batch-exec (non-slot) - PR#8407 CLOSED
STALE DETACHED HEAD WORKTREES (8 non-slot)
7 on master (safe to remove), 1 (doc-cleanup-plan-backend-7973) needs checking.
show me a table of these worktrees
∴ Thinking…
The user wants to see a table of the blocking worktrees and stale detached HEAD worktrees. Let me gather current info on all of them.
⏺ Bash(cat <<'SCRIPT' > /tmp/show_worktrees.sh #!/bin/bash cd /Users/schrockn/code/erk
echo "=== BLOCKING WORKTREES ===" printf "%-22s | %-55s | %-8s | %s\n" "Worktree" "Branch" "PR" "Last Commit" printf "%-22s-+-%-55s-+-%-8s-+-%s\n" "----------------------" "-------------------------------------------------------" "--------" "--------------------------------------------"
for info in \ "erk-slot-37|plnd/simplify-learn-land-trigge-02-25-1642|#8195 CLOSED" \ "erk-slot-54|plnd/O7724-rename-plan-number-8-02-26-2139|#8358 CLOSED" \ "erk-slot-55|plnd/split-commit-two-prs-02-27-1752|#8431 CLOSED" \ ; do slot=$(echo "$info" | cut -d'|' -f1) branch=$(echo "$info" | cut -d'|' -f2) pr=$(echo "$info" | cut -d'|' -f3) last=$(git log -1 --format="%s (%cr)" "$branch" 2>/dev/null | head -c 50) printf "%-22s | %-55s | %-8s | %s\n" "$slot" "$branch" "$pr" "$last" done
# Non-slot blocking branch="plnd/migrate-to-batch-exec-02-27-1155" last=$(git log -1 --format="%s (%cr)" "$branch" 2>/dev/null | head -c 50) printf "%-22s | %-55s | %-8s | %s\n" "migrate-to-batch-exec" "$branch" "#8407 CLOSED" "$last"
echo "" echo "=== STALE DETACHED HEAD WORKTREES ===" printf "%-45s | %-12s | %-10s | %s\n" "Worktree" "Commit" "On Master" "Commit Message" printf "%-45s-+-%-12s-+-%-10s-+-%s\n" "---------------------------------------------" "------------" "----------" "--------------------------------------------"
for name in d-flag doc-cleanup-plan-backend-7973 erk-br-co replace-session-gist-archi-02-21-1117 plan-unify-plan-next-st-02-21-2015 block-type-registry-02-28-0854 entity-store-kv-log-02-28-1104 immutable-entities-skill-02-28-0929; do path="/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/$name" # Handle plnd/ prefix paths if [ ! -d "$path" ]; then path="/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/plnd/$name" fi # Handle plan/ prefix paths if [ ! -d "$path" ]; then path="/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/plan/$name" fi # Handle planned/ prefix paths if [ ! -d "$path" ]; then path="/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/planned/$name" fi
commit=$(git -C "$path" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "???") on_master=$(git merge-base --is-ancestor "$commit" master 2>/dev/null && echo "YES" || echo "NO") msg=$(git log -1 --format="%s (%cr)" "$commit" 2>/dev/null | head -c 50) printf "%-45s | %-12s | %-10s | %s\n" "$name" "$commit" "$on_master" "$msg" done SCRIPT bash /tmp/show_worktrees.sh) ⎿ === BLOCKING WORKTREES === Worktree | Branch | PR | Last Commit -----------------------+---------------------------------------------------------+----------+--------------------------------------------- erk-slot-37 | plnd/simplify-learn-land-trigge-02-25-1642 | #8195 CLOSED | Add plan: Plan: Simplify Learn Trigger in `erk lan erk-slot-54 | plnd/O7724-rename-plan-number-8-02-26-2139 | #8358 CLOSED | WIP: Prepare for PR submission (3 days ago) erk-slot-55 | plnd/split-commit-two-prs-02-27-1752 | #8431 CLOSED | Remove .erk/impl-context/ before implementation (3 migrate-to-batch-exec | plnd/migrate-to-batch-exec-02-27-1155 | #8407 CLOSED | Migrate exec callers to batch variants and simplif
=== STALE DETACHED HEAD WORKTREES === Worktree | Commit | On Master | Commit Message ----------------------------------------------+--------------+------------+--------------------------------------------- d-flag | 68f782d59 | YES | Eliminate checkout cycles in plan_migrate_to_draft doc-cleanup-plan-backend-7973 | ca78c8e9c | NO | Update learned docs for draft-PR plan backend hard erk-br-co | 70807e213 | YES | changelog-updated (7 days ago) replace-session-gist-archi-02-21-1117 | 533d127c6 | YES | Fix objective update missing after land for plan/ plan-unify-plan-next-st-02-21-2015 | dedcb0d1f | YES | Fix ci-update-pr-body plan-header metadata preserv block-type-registry-02-28-0854 | 0ca0e8ec6 | YES | Add `/local:pr-triage` command for open PR analysi entity-store-kv-log-02-28-1104 | 2ed31d082 | YES | Add session preprocessing stats to `erk land` disc immutable-entities-skill-02-28-0929 | 0ca0e8ec6 | YES | Add `/local:pr-triage` command for open PR analysi
⏺ BLOCKING WORKTREES (4)
┌───────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Worktree │ Branch │ PR │ Last Commit │ ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ erk-slot-37 │ plnd/simplify-learn-land-trigge-02-25-1642 │ #8195 CLOSED │ Plan: Simplify Learn Trigger │ ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ erk-slot-54 │ plnd/O7724-rename-plan-number-8-02-26-2139 │ #8358 CLOSED │ WIP: Prepare for PR submission (3d) │ ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ erk-slot-55 │ plnd/split-commit-two-prs-02-27-1752 │ #8431 CLOSED │ Remove .erk/impl-context/ (3d) │ ├───────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ migrate-to-batch-exec │ plnd/migrate-to-batch-exec-02-27-1155 │ #8407 CLOSED │ Migrate exec callers to batch │ └───────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────┘
STALE DETACHED HEAD WORKTREES (8)
┌───────────────────────────────────────┬───────────┬──────────────────────────────────┐ │ Worktree │ On Master │ Commit Message │ ├───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤ │ d-flag │ YES │ Eliminate checkout cycles │ ├───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤ │ doc-cleanup-plan-backend-7973 │ NO │ Update learned docs for draft-PR │ ├───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤ │ erk-br-co │ YES │ changelog-updated (7d) │ ├───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤ │ replace-session-gist-archi-02-21-1117 │ YES │ Fix objective update │ ├───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤ │ plan-unify-plan-next-st-02-21-2015 │ YES │ Fix ci-update-pr-body │ ├───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤ │ block-type-registry-02-28-0854 │ YES │ Add pr-triage command │ ├───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤ │ entity-store-kv-log-02-28-1104 │ YES │ Add session preprocessing stats │ ├───────────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼──────────────────────────────────┤ │ immutable-entities-skill-02-28-0929 │ YES │ Add pr-triage command │ └───────────────────────────────────────┴───────────┴──────────────────────────────────┘
The only one needing caution is doc-cleanup-plan-backend-7973 (not on master). The other 7 detached HEAD worktrees are all on commits already in master.
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ❯ erk-slot-37 | plnd/simplify-learn-land-trigge-02-25-1642 | #8195 CLOSED | Add plan: Plan: Simplify Learn Trigger in `erk lan erk-slot-54 | plnd/O7724-rename-plan-number-8-02-26-2139 | #8358 CLOSED | WIP: Prepare for PR submission (3 days ago) erk-slot-55 | plnd/split-commit-two-prs-02-27-1752 | #8431 CLOSED | Remove .erk/impl-context/ before implementation (3

1. Unassign the 3 erk slots (37, 54, 55) that have closed PRs 2. Modify the `audit-branches` command to be aware of unassigning slots
Let me first understand how slots work in erk and how the audit-branches command works. I need to explore the codebase.

tool_use.name: Agent

FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 212 chars]...urrently does and how it works
Search in .claude/commands/ for audit-branches or similar.

session.tool_result:
Perfect! I now have the complete understanding of the `/local:audit-branches` command. Let me provide you with a comprehensive summary.
## Overview of `/local:audit-branches`
The `/local:audit-branches` command is a sophisticated branch audit workflow that helps identify and clean up stale branches, worktrees, and PRs. It's a multi-phase, context-aware process designed to help manage repository hygiene.
### What It Does (11-Phase Workflow)
**Phase 1: Data Collection** - Gathers all PRs (open/closed), worktrees, local branches, remote branches, and commit info - Detects worktree anomalies (duplicates, branch mismatches)
**Phase 2: Analysis** - Uses **context-based staleness** (NOT just age thresholds) - Identifies: superseded PRs, duplicates, abandoned WIP, outdated designs, pivoted features, massively diverged branches, and features merged differently - Age > 2 weeks is only a soft signal to investigate, not automatic cleanup
**Phase 3-5: Deep Analysis** - Analyzes worktrees without PRs by examining unique commits - For local-only branches: categorizes based on relationship to master - For uncertain branches: examines actual code changes to understand intent
**Phase 6: Blocking Worktree Detection** - **CRITICAL**: Identifies branches with closed/merged PRs that are currently checked out in worktrees - These block automated cleanup via `gt repo sync`
**Phase 7: Categorization** Results are organized into 6 categories:
| Category | Purpose |
... [47 lines omitted] ...

Excellent! I have a comprehensive understanding of how erk slots work. Let me compile the final report.
## Summary: How Erk Slots Work
Based on my analysis of the codebase and documentation, here's a complete picture of how erk slots function:
---
### 1. **Slot Assignment/Unassignment Commands**
**Commands for managing slots:**
| Command | Description | |---------|-------------| | `erk slot assign BRANCH` | Assign an **existing** branch to an available pool slot (fails if branch doesn't exist) | | `erk slot unassign [WORKTREE]` | Remove a branch assignment from a slot, check out placeholder branch | | `erk slot list` / `erk slot ls` | Display all pool slots with status, assignment, and health info | | `erk slot init-pool` | Initialize the worktree pool (first-time setup) | | `erk slot repair` | Auto-fix inconsistencies between pool state and filesystem |
**Implicit slot assignment via other commands:** - `erk branch create` — Creates new branch and assigns to slot - `erk branch checkout` — Assigns existing branch to slot - `erk pr checkout` — Assigns branch when checking out PR - `erk wt create-from` — Assigns branch to new slot
**Code references:** - Assign: `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/slot/assign_cmd.py` - Unassign: `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/slot/unassign_cmd.py` - List: `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/slot/list_cmd.py`
... [210 lines omitted] ...
└─ If non-interactive: error - **Status** — `available`, `assigned` (healthy), or `error`
session.thinking:
Now I have a good understanding of both systems. Let me:
1. First, unassign the 3 slots (37, 54, 55) - but wait, I'm in plan mode, so I can't execute anything. The user asked me to both do an action AND plan a code change.
Actually, re-reading the user's message: "unassign the slots and change audit-branches to be aware of unassigning slots"
Two parts: 1. Unassign the slots (erk-slot-37, erk-slot-54, erk-slot-55) - this is an action
2. Change audit-branches to be aware of unassigning slots - this is a code change
Since I'm in plan mode, I'll focus on planning the audit-branches modification while noting that the slot unassignment will be part of the implementation. First, I need to examine the audit-branches command to understand what needs to change.
session.assistant: Let me read the audit-branches command to understand what needs to change.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→description: Audit and clean up stale branches, worktrees, and PRs 3→--- 4→ 5→# /audit-branches 6→ 7→Automates the branch audit workflow to identify stale branches, worktrees, and PRs that should be cleaned up. 8→ 9→## Usage 10→ 11→```bash 12→/audit-branches 13→``` 14→ 15→## What This Command Does 16→ 17→1. **Collects data** about PRs, worktrees, and branches 18→2. **Analyzes staleness** based on context (not just age) 19→3. **Presents categorized recommendations** for cleanup 20→4. **Allows user selection** of what to clean 21→5. **Executes cleanup** with confirmation before destructive operations 22→ 23→--- 24→ 25→## Agent Instructions 26→ 27→You are executing a branch audit workflow. Follow these phases carefully. 28→ 29→**Shell Compatibility Note:** Complex bash pipelines may not work correctly in zsh. For multi-step scripts, write to a temporary file and execute with `bash`: 30→
... [583 lines omitted] ...
598→## Error Handling 600→- If a branch deletion fails, continue with others and report failures at end
session.thinking:
Now I understand the full picture. The user wants two things:
1. **Unassign slots 37, 54, 55** - These are blocking worktrees with closed PRs 2. **Update audit-branches command** to automatically unassign blocking slot worktrees (instead of just reporting them)
Looking at the current audit-branches command, in Phase 10, Step 1, it already has logic to free blocking worktrees - but only when the user selects that option in Phase 9. The user wants the command to be more proactive about this.
Let me think about what change makes sense. The current flow is:
1. Detect blocking worktrees (Phase 6) 2. Present them (Phase 8) 3. Ask user what to do (Phase 9) 4. Execute if selected (Phase 10)
I think the user wants blocking slot worktrees with closed PRs to be automatically unassigned as part of the cleanup flow, similar to how AUTO-CLEANUP branches work. These are clearly stale - the PR is closed, there's no reason to keep the slot occupied. So I need to make this automatic rather than optional, and I also need to unassign those three specific slots right now.
session.assistant: Now I have a clear picture. Let me write the plan.

tool_use.param:

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/async-kindling-dragonfly.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Unassign blocking slots and teach audit-branches to auto-unassign Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create a plan PR" (Recommended) - Create a draft PR with the plan and stop. Does NOT proceed to implementation. 2. "Skip PR and implement here" - Skip creating a PR, implement directly in current worktree (for small PR iterations that don't need PR tracking). 3. "View/Edit the plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage editing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create a plan PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. STOP - Do NOT call ExitPlanMode. The plan-save command handles everything. Stay in plan mode and let the user exit manually if desired.
If user chooses 'Skip PR and implement here': 1. Create implement-now marker (skip PR creation): erk exec marker create --session-id 2328dfe2-dd5a-48c7-adab-05a6e8a502d0 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly
... [7 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Here's the plan:
---
## Plan: Unassign blocking slots and teach audit-branches to auto-unassign
### Context
Three erk slots (37, 54, 55) are occupied by branches with closed PRs, blocking `gt repo sync` cleanup. Currently, audit-branches *detects* blocking slot worktrees but only acts on them if the user manually selects that category in Phase 9. Slot worktrees with closed PRs are unambiguously stale — the slot should be freed automatically, just like AUTO-CLEANUP branches.
### Step 1: Unassign the 3 blocking slots
```bash erk slot unassign erk-slot-37 erk slot unassign erk-slot-54 erk slot unassign erk-slot-55 ```
If any fail due to uncommitted changes, discard changes first, then retry.
### Step 2: Update audit-branches command
**File:** `.claude/commands/local/audit-branches.md`
1. **Phase 7 (Categorization):** Split blocking worktrees into: - **Blocking slot worktrees** (erk-slot-NN with closed PRs) → move to AUTO-CLEANUP, auto-unassign - **Blocking non-slot worktrees** (vanilla worktrees with closed PRs) → keep in BLOCKING WORKTREES for user confirmation
2. **Phase 10 (Execution):** In Step 0 (AUTO-CLEANUP), add slot unassignment — run `erk slot unassign <slot>`, handle dirty worktrees, then `gt repo sync` cleans up freed branches.
3. **Phase 8 (Present Findings):** Update tables to show slot worktrees as auto-cleanup items; only non-slot blocking worktrees require user decision.
### Verification
Run `/local:audit-branches` and confirm slot worktrees are auto-unassigned while non-slot blocking worktrees still require user confirmation.
---

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Plan: Unassign blocking slots and teach audit-branches to auto-unassign\n...[truncated 455 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Plan: Unassign blocking slots and teach audit-branches to auto-unassign Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create a plan PR (Recommended)". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type` and `--objective` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, branch slug, summary, and optional flags:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="2328dfe2-dd5a-48c7-adab-05a6e8a502d0" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "2328dfe2-dd5a-48c7-adab-05a6e8a502d0" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
If `branch_name` is present in the JSON, display the same next-steps block as the success case below.
If `branch_name` is absent, display only: `View PR: <plan_url>`
Return immediately (skip Steps 5, 6 above if not already executed).
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
**Slot options block:**
The "OR exit Claude Code first" section should show both slot allocation options, with the recommended one listed first based on trunk detection:
If **on trunk = true**:
``` OR exit Claude Code first, then run one of:
New slot (recommended — you're on trunk): Local: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan <plan_number> Implement: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan <plan_number> --script)" && erk implement --dangerous
Same slot: Local: erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> Implement: source "$(erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> --script)" && erk implement --dangerous
Dispatch to Queue: erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If **on trunk = false**:
``` OR exit Claude Code first, then run one of:
Same slot (recommended — you're in a slot): Local: erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> Implement: source "$(erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> --script)" && erk implement --dangerous
New slot: Local: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan <plan_number> Implement: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan <plan_number> --script)" && erk implement --dangerous
Dispatch to Queue: erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
**Next steps block:**
``` Next steps:
View PR: <plan_url>
In Claude Code: Dispatch to queue: /erk:pr-dispatch — Dispatch plan for remote agent implementation
OR exit Claude Code first, then run one of: Checkout: erk br co --for-plan <plan_number> Dispatch to Queue: erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
If the JSON output contains `slot_name` and `slot_objective_updated: true`, also display: `Slot objective updated: <slot_name> → #<objective-number>`
**Note:** Slot objective updates are handled automatically by `plan-save` when an objective is linked via the session marker - no separate command call needed.
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "2328dfe2-dd5a-48c7-adab-05a6e8a502d0" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
Let me execute the plan-save workflow.
First, let me read the plan file to get the title.
session.tool_result:
1→# Plan: Unassign blocking slots and teach audit-branches to auto-unassign 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→Three erk slots (37, 54, 55) are occupied by branches with closed PRs, blocking `gt repo sync` cleanup. Currently, audit-branches *detects* blocking slot worktrees but only acts on them if the user manually selects that category in Phase 9. Slot worktrees with closed PRs are unambiguously stale — the slot should be freed automatically, just like AUTO-CLEANUP branches. 6→ 7→## Step 1: Unassign the 3 blocking slots 8→ 9→```bash 10→erk slot unassign erk-slot-37 11→erk slot unassign erk-slot-54 12→erk slot unassign erk-slot-55 13→``` 14→ 15→If any fail due to uncommitted changes, discard changes first: 16→```bash 17→cd <worktree-path> && git checkout . && git clean -fd 18→erk slot unassign <slot> 19→``` 20→ 21→## Step 2: Update audit-branches command 22→ 23→**File:** `.claude/commands/local/audit-branches.md` 24→ 25→**Change:** Move blocking slot worktrees from the interactive "ask user" flow to automatic execution. Specifically: 26→ 27→1. **Phase 7 (Categorization):** Split the current `BLOCKING WORKTREES` category into two: 28→ - **Blocking slot worktrees** (erk-slot-NN with closed PRs) → move to AUTO-CLEANUP, auto-unassign 29→ - **Blocking non-slot worktrees** (vanilla worktrees with closed PRs) → keep in BLOCKING WORKTREES for user confirmation 30→
... [14 lines omitted] ...
session.thinking:
Title: "Plan: Unassign blocking slots and teach audit-branches to auto-unassign"
Branch slug: "auto-unassign-blocking-slots" (26 chars, good)
Plan summary: "Slot worktrees with closed PRs are unambiguously stale and should be auto-unassigned during audit-branches cleanup. This updates the audit-branches command to automatically free blocking slot worktrees instead of requiring user confirmation, while keeping non-slot blocking worktrees in the interactive flow."
No --plan-type or --objective flags.
session.assistant:
Now let me save the plan. The title is "Unassign blocking slots and teach audit-branches to auto-unassign".
- **Branch slug:** `auto-unassign-blocking-slots` - **Plan summary:** Slot worktrees with closed PRs are unambiguously stale and should be auto-unassigned during audit-branches cleanup. This updates the audit-branches command to automatically free blocking slot worktrees instead of requiring user confirmation, while keeping non-slot blocking worktrees in the interactive flow.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="2328dfe2-dd5a-48c7-adab-05a6e8a502d0" --bra...[truncated 250 chars]...ing user confirmation, while keeping non-slot blocking worktrees in the interactive flow."
Save plan as draft PR via erk
